seo.title: Recurring Billing & Subscription Invoicing | Invoicemonk
seo.description: Automate recurring invoices for retainer clients, subscriptions, and repeat services. Set it up once and get paid on schedule. Free tier available.

hero.title: Automate your recurring invoices
hero.description: Set up billing schedules for retainer clients, subscriptions, and monthly services. Invoicemonk sends invoices automatically so you never miss a billing cycle.

overview.title: Why recurring billing matters for your business
paragraphs: Recurring billing is the backbone of predictable revenue. Whether you run a marketing agency billing retainer clients monthly, a SaaS company collecting subscription fees, or a consultancy with ongoing engagements, the ability to automate your invoicing cycle eliminates manual work and ensures you get paid consistently. Without automation, businesses lose an average of 5–10 hours per month on repetitive invoicing tasks — time better spent serving clients and growing revenue.

The challenge with manual recurring billing isn't just the time cost — it's the errors. Missed invoices, incorrect amounts, forgotten tax adjustments, and inconsistent payment terms erode client trust and create accounting headaches. A single missed billing cycle can delay cash flow by 30–60 days, especially when clients use the oversight as an excuse to defer payment.

Invoicemonk's recurring billing solves these problems by letting you define a schedule once and automating everything from invoice generation to delivery and payment reminders. Every invoice is numbered sequentially, tax-compliant, and branded with your business identity — exactly as if you had created it manually, but without lifting a finger.

steps.title: 1. Set a schedule
steps.desc: Choose weekly, monthly, quarterly, or custom intervals. Define start and optional end dates for each billing cycle.

steps.title: 2. Invoices auto-generate
steps.desc: Invoicemonk creates and sends each invoice on schedule with the correct line items, taxes, and payment terms.

steps.title: 3. Track payments
steps.desc: Monitor payment status, outstanding balances, and recurring revenue trends in real time from your dashboard.

features.title: How Invoicemonk makes recurring billing effortless
features.subtitle: Every feature designed to save you time and protect your revenue

items.title: Flexible Schedules
items.desc: Weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, or yearly — set any billing frequency that matches your client agreements. Define start dates, end dates, and even skip specific periods.

items.title: Automatic Reminders
items.desc: Clients receive professional payment reminders before and after due dates. Configure reminder intervals or use smart defaults that optimize payment collection.

items.title: Tax Compliance
items.desc: Every recurring invoice includes the correct tax rates for your jurisdiction. As tax rules change, update your rates once and all future invoices reflect the new amounts.

items.title: Per-Client Customization
items.desc: Each client can have unique line items, amounts, currencies, and payment terms. No two recurring schedules need to be the same — full flexibility for diverse client relationships.

items.title: Client Portal Access
items.desc: Clients can view their recurring invoice history, download past invoices, and verify payment status through their dedicated portal — reducing support inquiries.

items.title: Instant Adjustments
items.desc: Need to change a retainer amount mid-contract? Update the schedule and changes apply from the next cycle. Previous invoices remain unchanged for accurate records.

example.title: Recurring billing in action
example.scenario: Scenario: A marketing agency with 12 retainer clients
paragraphs: Sarah runs a digital marketing agency with 12 monthly retainer clients ranging from ₦150,000 to ₦1,200,000 per month. Before Invoicemonk, she spent the first two days of every month manually creating invoices, copying line items, and adjusting payment terms for each client.

With Invoicemonk's recurring billing, Sarah set up 12 schedules in under an hour. Each schedule has the client's specific retainer amount, preferred currency, and payment terms (Net 15 for most, Net 30 for two enterprise clients). On the 1st of every month, all 12 invoices generate and deliver automatically.

The result: Sarah recovered 16+ hours per month in administrative time, reduced late payments by 40% thanks to automatic reminders, and her monthly recurring revenue is now tracked in real-time on her dashboard.

items.question: How does recurring billing work in Invoicemonk?
items.answer: Set a schedule (weekly, monthly, quarterly, or custom) and Invoicemonk automatically generates, numbers, and sends invoices to your clients on time.

items.question: Can I customize each recurring invoice?
items.answer: Yes. Every recurring schedule can have its own line items, amounts, currency, tax rates, and payment terms.

items.question: Will clients receive automatic reminders for recurring invoices?
items.answer: Absolutely. Payment reminders work the same way for recurring invoices — clients receive notifications before and after due dates.

items.question: Can I pause or stop a recurring schedule?
items.answer: Yes. Pause, modify, or cancel any recurring invoice at any time. All previously generated invoices remain in your records.

items.question: Does recurring billing support multiple currencies?
items.answer: Yes. Each recurring schedule can be set in any currency Invoicemonk supports.

items.question: Can I set different billing frequencies for different clients?
items.answer: Each client can have their own unique recurring schedule. Bill one client weekly and another quarterly.

items.question: What happens if a recurring invoice goes unpaid?
items.answer: Invoicemonk tracks the payment status of every invoice. Unpaid recurring invoices appear in your outstanding receivables and trigger automatic payment reminders.

items.question: Is there a limit to how many recurring schedules I can create?
items.answer: Free plans support up to 3 active recurring schedules. Paid plans support unlimited schedules.
